qa_pairs.question: What kind of commercial software packages are not externally available through the CVMFS client setup described on this page?
qa_pairs.answer: Commercial software packages such as the Intel and Portland Group compilers are not available externally, even when following the CVMFS client setup instructions on this page, due to licensing terms.

qa_pairs.question: If modules for commercial compilers like Intel and PGI are available, what specific parts of these packages can users access externally?
qa_pairs.answer: Users will only have access to the redistributable parts of these packages, typically the shared objects, even when the modules for Intel and PGI compilers are available.

qa_pairs.question: What is the limitation of only having access to the redistributable parts of commercial compilers like Intel and PGI?
qa_pairs.answer: While the redistributable parts (shared objects) are sufficient for running software packages already compiled with these compilers, they are not adequate for compiling new software.
